channel.title: +Articles on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Rationality and normative inversion
item.description: In chapter 2 of @jsaideepak ’s book, he draws our attention to the concepts of Modernity and Rationality that were used by the European colonizers as whetstones for validity of concepts and practices. Interesting to see this normative inversion with respect to Lindy’s Principle. You might have come across statements like “This is the 21st century. Why are you still sporting a religious mark on your forehead?” The hidden assumption here is that sporting religious marks is a pre-modern practice.

item.title: Revolutionaries and Intellectuals
item.description: Source - OGS16 Q: What is it that intellectuals find so attractive about Maoist, Naxalite, and separatist ideologies? A: They are intellectual in origin. .. Redner shows that intellectuals have literally intellectualized the world, resulting in people being disenchanted with it.+++(4)+++ From cultural dimensions like art, religion, ethics, politics, all the way to philosophy and so on, intellectualization has degraded it all. Which is precisely why intellectuals tend to be hated and despised by their own people because the people often find them disgraceful.
